extra.reasoning: The property requires that flipping abdominal_pain false->true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. It is violated by the high-priority interaction_drop rule. Witness input: palpitations=vomiting=stiff_neck=vision_loss=abdominal_pain=true, fever=focal_weakness=slurred_speech=false. BEFORE (abdominal_pain=false, all else as above): interaction_drop does not fire (it needs all five of palpitations, vomiting, stiff_neck, vision_loss, abdominal_pain). Firing acuity rules: raise_vomiting(p3->emergent), raise_vision_loss(p3->emergent), raise_stiff_neck(p2->urgent), raise_palpitations(p2->urgent). Highest priority is 3, so acuity=emergent. AFTER (abdominal_pain=true): now all five conjuncts hold, so interaction_drop (priority 100) fires, setting acuity:=self_care; priority 100 beats the priority-3 emergent rules, so acuity=self_care. Setting abdominal_pain true thus lowers acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment records the AFTER state (abdominal_pain=true); the comparison BEFORE state is identical except abdominal_pain=false.
